task.title: Fix: approval buttons highlight after approval

messages.content:
## Task

Fix approval buttons in the S-Deck UI so that after the operator approves, the clicked button is shown in a highlighted (active/selected) color.

## Context

Approval buttons appear on messages that have `needs_approval: true`. When the operator clicks Approve, the bus reply is sent — but the button currently has no visual feedback showing it was pressed.

The fix: after the operator clicks an approval button, visually highlight it (e.g. filled primary color, disabled state) so it's clear which option was selected.

## Where to look

- `public/app.js` and/or `public/index.html` — the S-Deck frontend
- Search for "approval", "needs_approval", "approve", or button rendering logic for messages
- The button likely renders from a message object with `needs_approval: true` and `request_options`

## What to change

When an approval button is clicked and the reply is sent:
1. Visually highlight the clicked button (e.g. add an `active` or `selected` CSS class, or change its style to filled/primary)
2. Optionally disable the other buttons (since only one option can be chosen)
3. The state should persist — if the message is re-rendered, the selected option stays highlighted

## Constraints

- Work inside `.synapse/worktrees/developer-33-approval-btn-highlight`
- Commit all changes there; do not push or merge
- Report DONE to me (your orchestrator) with commit SHA and files changed

The keyboard shortcut changes were applied directly to the working tree on `main` — not committed to any branch or worktree. My fault for skipping the worktree step.

git log --oneline -3:
```
0fdd0c9 fix(protocol): workers send DONE only to orchestrator via report_done
726a5e0 fix(restart): derive orchestrator ID as <projectId>:0 instead of querying messages
78d2155 chore(tests): add eval case for task 107
```
Current branch: main

The changes are still uncommitted in the working tree. Should I commit them to main, stage them into the approval-button worktree, or discard them?

The keyboard shortcut changes look good. Please do the following:

1. From the main working tree, stash the changes: `git stash`
2. `cd` into your active worktree at `.synapse/worktrees/developer-33-approval-btn-highlight`
3. Pop the stash there: `git stash pop`
4. Commit those keyboard shortcut changes as a separate commit in the worktree with message: `feat(ui): Enter to send, Shift+Enter newline, Alt+Enter urgent`

Then continue with the approval button highlight task. Report DONE to me when both changes are committed.
